market_baseline: Bearish — SPY up slightly in premarket (+0.20% at $672.89) but futures hit by weak NFP data and geopolitical strikes in Iran; VIX up 16% to 27.54.

ai_word_of_the_day.definition: AI systems that can perform complex tasks autonomously with minimal human intervention, shifting from simple chatbots to autonomous teammates.

items.content: S&P 500 futures sink as Nonfarm Payrolls miss expectations and oil prices rise amidst geopolitical tension. SPY premarket at $672.89 (+0.20%).
VIX surged 15.96% to 27.54 as market uncertainty spikes following military strikes in Iran and economic data concerns.
MSFT trading at $410.20 (-0.12%) in premarket. Analysts keep 'Strong Buy' but cite short-term declining trend risks.
INTC down 1.39% in premarket at $45.31. AI server chip demand remains strong but US lawmakers raise flags over Chinese-linked supplier ties.
OpenAI signs deal to deploy models on Pentagon classified network. Sam Altman defends move stating government power should exceed private companies.
Lenny Rachitsky explores how AI agents are reducing PM 'busywork'. 2026 trends include 200k token context windows and native multimodal support.
Marty Cagan argues strategic PM roles are safe while 'product owner' delivery roles face automation. Focus must shift to value and viability.
OpenAI scales back direct booking features in ChatGPT, pushing traffic back to Expedia and Booking.com, boosting OTA stock sentiment.
Expedia shifts focus to site speed and conversion rates. Partnership with PredictHQ brings predictive demand intelligence for major events like FIFA 2026.
90% of hoteliers plan large-scale AI deployment for guest messaging and revenue management by 2026. Contactless tech is now an industry standard.
